iversity of California, Berkeley Technical Report No. UCB/EECS-2008-140 October 28, 2008 http://www2.eecs.berkeley.edu/Pubs/TechRpts/2008/EECS-2008-140.pdf It is becoming increasingly popular to describe real time signal proessing systems t

to describe real time signal proessing systems targetted for FPGA or ASIC implementation using structural signal flow graphs. We have implemented support for genera- tion of synthesizeable as well as testbench VHDL code from Ptolemy II models.

l as testbench VHDL code from Ptolemy II models. A helper based approach bor- rowing heavily from the existing Ptolemy II C code generation framework is used. This work demon- strates the extensibility of the helper based code- generation approa

ility of the helper based code- generation approach and sets the stage for future re- search in synthesis of efficient hardware descriptions from heterogenous visual models. BibTeX citation: @techreport{Leung:EECS-2008-140, Author= {Leung, Man
